Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a Python dictionary representing a Log Skeleton process model for a given order fulfillment scenario The Log Skeleton has specific declarative constraints equivalence always before always after never together activ freq and directly follows

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Constraints and Format equivalence Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs it has the same occurrences as B always before Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must have occurred before it always after Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must occur after it never together Set of tuples A B meaning A and B cannot coexist in the same case activ freq Dictionary mapping activity to a set of allowed occurrence counts directly follows Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must occur immediately after

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Scenario and Activities Activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Process flow described RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D to IC to RP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The prompt implies a mostly sequential flow I will assume each activity happens exactly once per order for simplicity The prompt does not mention splits loops or optional paths explicitly so I will model it as a strict sequence where each activity occurs exactly once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Derive Constraints from the Sequence Activity Occurrences activ freq Each activity happens exactly once per case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows based on the sequence RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC Note format is first second where if first occurs second was before

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Equivalence Since each happens exactly once all activities are equivalent in terms of occurrence count I will list sequential pairs to imply it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never Together Since it is a single linear process without alternative branches there are no mutually exclusive activities so this set will be empty

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check always before tuple order If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously so CA RO means if CA occurs RO was before correct Check always after tuple order RO CA means if RO occurs CA is after correct Check directly follows tuple order RO CA correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Is there any chance some activities are optional When a new customer order arrives it must be processed through a series of activities implies mandatory so 1 for all is correct

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Should I include transitive relations for always before and always after In academic Log Skeleton definitions these are often computed over the whole log and include transitive pairs However for a constructed model from a description direct relations are standard and sufficient I will stick to direct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Everything aligns activ freq all 1 never together empty equivalence paired always before always after and directly follows match the prompt definitions
